verses.text: “Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might, and the beginning of my strength; excelling in dignity, and excelling in power.

verses.name: Genesis 49:4
verses.text: Boiling over like water, you shall not excel; because you went up to your father’s bed, then defiled it. He went up to my couch.

verses.name: Genesis 49:5
verses.text: “Simeon and Levi are brothers. Their swords are weapons of violence.

verses.name: Genesis 49:6
verses.text: My soul, don’t come into their council. My glory, don’t be united to their assembly; for in their anger they killed men. In their self-will they hamstrung cattle.

verses.name: Genesis 49:7
verses.text: Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce; and their wrath, for it was cruel. I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel.

verses.name: Genesis 49:8
verses.text: “Judah, your brothers will praise you. Your hand will be on the neck of your enemies. Your father’s sons will bow down before you.

verses.name: Genesis 49:9
verses.text: Judah is a lion’s cub. From the prey, my son, you have gone up. He stooped down, he crouched as a lion, as a lioness. Who will rouse him up?

verses.name: Genesis 49:10
verses.text: The scepter will not depart from Judah, nor the ruler’s staff from between his feet, until he comes to whom it belongs. To him will the obedience of the peoples be.

verses.name: Genesis 49:11
verses.text: Binding his foal to the vine, his donkey’s colt to the choice vine; he has washed his garments in wine, his robes in the blood of grapes.

verses.name: Genesis 49:12
verses.text: His eyes will be red with wine, his teeth white with milk.

verses.name: Genesis 49:13
verses.text: “Zebulun will dwell at the haven of the sea. He will be for a haven of ships. His border will be on Sidon.

verses.name: Genesis 49:14
verses.text: “Issachar is a strong donkey, lying down between the saddlebags.

verses.name: Genesis 49:15
verses.text: He saw a resting place, that it was good, the land, that it was pleasant. He bows his shoulder to the burden, and becomes a servant doing forced labor.

verses.name: Genesis 49:16
verses.text: “Dan will judge his people, as one of the tribes of Israel.

verses.name: Genesis 49:17
verses.text: Dan will be a serpent on the trail, an adder in the path, That bites the horse’s heels, so that his rider falls backward.

verses.name: Genesis 49:18
verses.text: I have waited for your salvation, Yahweh.

verses.name: Genesis 49:19
verses.text: “A troop will press on Gad, but he will press on their heel.

verses.name: Genesis 49:20
verses.text: “Asher’s food will be rich. He will yield royal dainties.

verses.name: Genesis 49:21
verses.text: “Naphtali is a doe set free, who bears beautiful fawns.

verses.name: Genesis 49:22
verses.text: “Joseph is a fruitful vine, a fruitful vine by a spring. His branches run over the wall.

verses.name: Genesis 49:23
verses.text: The archers have severely grieved him, shot at him, and persecute him:

verses.name: Genesis 49:24
verses.text: But his bow remained strong. The arms of his hands were made strong, by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, (from there is the shepherd, the stone of Israel),

verses.name: Genesis 49:25
verses.text: even by the God of your father, who will help you; by the Almighty, who will bless you, with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that lies below, blessings of the breasts, and of the womb.

verses.name: Genesis 49:26
verses.text: The blessings of your father have prevailed above the blessings of your ancestors, above the boundaries of the ancient hills. They will be on the head of Joseph, on the crown of the head of him who is separated from his brothers.

verses.name: Genesis 49:27
verses.text: “Benjamin is a ravenous wolf. In the morning he will devour the prey. At evening he will divide the plunder.”

verses.name: Genesis 49:28
verses.text: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father spoke to them and blessed them. He blessed everyone according to his blessing.

verses.name: Genesis 49:29
verses.text: He instructed them, and said to them, “I am to be gathered to my people. Bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,

verses.name: Genesis 49:30
verses.text: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite as a burial place.

verses.name: Genesis 49:31
verses.text: There they buried Abraham and Sarah, his wife. There they buried Isaac and Rebekah, his wife, and there I buried Leah:

verses.name: Genesis 49:32
verses.text: the field and the cave that is therein, which was purchased from the children of Heth.”

verses.name: Genesis 49:33
verses.text: When Jacob made an end of charging his sons, he gathered up his feet into the bed, and yielded up the spirit, and was gathered to his people.

verses.name: Genesis 50:1
verses.text: Joseph fell on his father’s face, wept on him, and kissed him.

verses.name: Genesis 50:2
verses.text: Joseph commanded his servants, the physicians, to embalm his father; and the physicians embalmed Israel.

verses.name: Genesis 50:3
verses.text: Forty days were fulfilled for him, for that is how many the days it takes to embalm. The Egyptians wept for him for seventy days.

verses.name: Genesis 50:4
verses.text: When the days of weeping for him were past, Joseph spoke to the house of Pharaoh, saying, “If now I have found favor in your eyes, please speak in the ears of Pharaoh, saying,

verses.name: Genesis 50:5
verses.text: ‘My father made me swear, saying, “Behold, I am dying. Bury me in my grave which I have dug for myself in the land of Canaan.” Now therefore, please let me go up and bury my father, and I will come again.’”

verses.name: Genesis 50:6
verses.text: Pharaoh said, “Go up, and bury your father, just like he made you swear.”

verses.name: Genesis 50:7
verses.text: Joseph went up to bury his father; and with him went up all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his house, all the elders of the land of Egypt,

verses.name: Genesis 50:8
verses.text: all the house of Joseph, his brothers, and his father’s house. Only their little ones, their flocks, and their herds, they left in the land of Goshen.

verses.name: Genesis 50:9
verses.text: There went up with him both chariots and horsemen. It was a very great company.

verses.name: Genesis 50:10
verses.text: They came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, and there they lamented with a very great and severe lamentation. He mourned for his father seven days.

verses.name: Genesis 50:11
verses.text: When the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning in the floor of Atad, they said, “This is a grievous mourning by the Egyptians.” Therefore its name was called Abel Mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.

verses.name: Genesis 50:12
verses.text: His sons did to him just as he commanded them,

verses.name: Genesis 50:13
verses.text: for his sons carried him into the land of Canaan, and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham bought with the field, for a possession of a burial site, from Ephron the Hittite, before Mamre.

verses.name: Genesis 50:14
verses.text: Joseph returned into Egypt—he, and his brothers, and all that went up with him to bury his father, after he had buried his father.

verses.name: Genesis 50:15
verses.text: When Joseph’s brothers saw that their father was dead, they said, “It may be that Joseph will hate us, and will fully pay us back for all of the evil which we did to him.”

verses.name: Genesis 50:16
verses.text: They sent a message to Joseph, saying, “Your father commanded before he died, saying,

verses.name: Genesis 50:17
verses.text: ‘You shall tell Joseph, “Now please forgive the disobedience of your brothers, and their sin, because they did evil to you.”’ Now, please forgive the disobedience of the servants of the God of your father.” Joseph wept when they spoke to him.

verses.name: Genesis 50:18
verses.text: His brothers also went and fell down before his face; and they said, “Behold, we are your servants.”

verses.name: Genesis 50:19
verses.text: Joseph said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for am I in the place of God?

verses.name: Genesis 50:20
verses.text: As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to bring to pass, as it is this day, to save many people alive.

verses.name: Genesis 50:21
verses.text: Now therefore don’t be afraid. I will nourish you and your little ones.” He comforted them, and spoke kindly to them.

verses.name: Genesis 50:22
verses.text: Joseph lived in Egypt, he, and his father’s house. Joseph lived one hundred ten years.

verses.name: Genesis 50:23
verses.text: Joseph saw Ephraim’s children to the third generation. The children also of Machir, the son of Manasseh, were born on Joseph’s knees.

verses.name: Genesis 50:24
verses.text: Joseph said to his brothers, “I am dying, but God will surely visit you, and bring you up out of this land to the land which he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.”

verses.name: Genesis 50:25
verses.text: Joseph took an oath of the children of Israel, saying, “God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.”

verses.name: Genesis 50:26
verses.text: So Joseph died, being one hundred ten years old, and they embalmed him, and he was put in a coffin in Egypt.
